introSection:
Memphis Kiddie Park is a children's amusement park located in Brooklyn, Ohio. The park opened on May 28, 1952. It was one of several designed by Stuart Wintner, who also owned a chain of movie theaters. Wintner and his son Russell ran the park until each of their deaths.

The park is home to the Little Dipper roller coaster, one of the oldest operating steel roller coasters in North America.

sections.title: Current rides
sections.content: There are 11 rides at Memphis Kiddie Park. All date back to the 1950s and have been maintained to keep their original appearances. In addition to the rides, the park also has a miniature golf course for both children and adults.

fullText:
Memphis Kiddie Park is a children's amusement park located in Brooklyn, Ohio. The park opened on May 28, 1952. It was one of several designed by Stuart Wintner, who also owned a chain of movie theaters. Wintner and his son Russell ran the park until each of their deaths.

The park is home to the Little Dipper roller coaster, one of the oldest operating steel roller coasters in North America.

There are 11 rides at Memphis Kiddie Park. All date back to the 1950s and have been maintained to keep their original appearances. In addition to the rides, the park also has a miniature golf course for both children and adults.





 * Memphis Kiddie Park
categories: Buildings and structures in Cuyahoga County, Ohio
